agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects payment processing, inventory management, fulfillment workflows, and customer communication. The PM's request is a single sentence with no constraints specified, leaving significant room for interpretation.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed customer purchase comprising one or more line items, payment authorization, and a fulfillment lifecycle."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an order (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A customer- or system-initiated action that terminates an order before final delivery, triggering refund and inventory reversal workflows."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of captured or authorized payment funds to the customer's original payment method following cancellation."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order states during which cancellation is permitted. Typically before the order has been shipped or handed off to a carrier."},{"term":"Partial Cancellation","definition":"Cancelling individual line items from a multi-item order while the remaining items proceed to fulfillment."},{"term":"Restocking","definition":"Returning cancelled order items to available inventory."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled when it is in a cancellable state (before physical shipment/handoff to carrier).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once goods have left the warehouse and are in carrier custody, cancellation is physically impossible — the correct process becomes a return/RMA, not a cancellation. Allowing cancellation post-shipment would corrupt fulfillment state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status in {Pending, Confirmed, Processing}, cancellation succeeds. Given an order with status in {Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled}, cancellation is rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Cancellation must trigger a full refund to the original payment method for the cancelled items.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Retaining payment for goods not delivered violates payment processing regulations and consumer protection law. A cancelled order with no refund leaves the financial ledger in an inconsistent state.","testable_assertion":"After successful cancellation, a refund record exists for 100% of the cancelled amount against the original payment instrument."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Cancelled items must be returned to available inventory.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failure to restock creates a permanent inventory leak — units are neither sold nor available, corrupting inventory counts.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation, each cancelled line item's quantity is added back to available inventory stock."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled order cannot transition to any state other than Cancelled (terminal state).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing a cancelled order to resurrect into Processing or Shipped would violate refund integrity and inventory consistency.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in Cancelled state, any attempt to transition it to another state is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The customer must receive confirmation of the cancellation and expected refund timeline.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a communication policy, not a data integrity constraint. The method and timing of notification could change with business policy.","testable_assertion":"After successful cancellation, a cancellation confirmation notification is enqueued for the customer within the configured SLA."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Only the order owner (or an authorized agent) may cancel the order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel others' orders is a security and data integrity violation.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request where the requesting user ID does not match the order owner ID (and the user lacks admin/agent role) is rejected with an authorization error."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has an order in 'Pending' or 'Confirmed' status","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The order status transitions to 'Cancelled', a full refund is initiated, inventory is restocked, and the customer receives a cancellation confirmation."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has an order in 'Processing' status (picked/packed but not yet shipped)","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to 'Cancelled', a full refund is initiated, inventory is restocked, and any in-progress fulfillment work is halted."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an order in 'Shipped' status","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a clear message explaining the order has already shipped and directing the customer to the returns process."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has an order in 'Delivered' status","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a message directing the customer to the returns/refund process."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer has an order already in 'Cancelled' status","when":"The customer requests cancellation again","then":"The system returns an idempotent success response indicating the order is already cancelled, without duplicating refunds or inventory adjustments."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer attempts to cancel an order belonging to a different customer","when":"The cancellation request is submitted","then":"The request is rejected with an authorization error. No state change occurs."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer cancels an order and the payment provider refund call fails","when":"The refund cannot be processed","then":"The order remains in its current state (NOT transitioned to Cancelled), an error is returned to the customer, and the failure is logged for operational follow-up."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A multi-item order where the customer wants to cancel only some items","when":"The customer requests partial cancellation","then":"The system either supports partial cancellation (cancelling selected line items, adjusting the refund proportionally, restocking only those items) or rejects partial cancellation with guidance to cancel the entire order — depending on business decision (see escalation)."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent cancellation and shipment: warehouse ships the order at the exact moment the customer submits a cancellation request.","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic locking or a check-then-act pattern on order status. If the order transitioned to Shipped before the cancellation lock was acquired, cancellation is rejected. The customer is informed the order has shipped and directed to returns."},{"scenario":"Payment refund fails after order is already marked Cancelled.","expected_behavior":"This must not happen — the order should only transition to Cancelled after refund initiation succeeds (or at minimum, is accepted by the payment provider). If using eventual consistency, a 'CancellationPending' intermediate state should be used, with reconciliation jobs to retry failed refunds."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order paid with multiple payment methods (e.g., gift card + credit card).","expected_behavior":"Refund must be split proportionally across the original payment instruments. Gift card balance is restored, credit card is refunded for the remainder."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that had a promotional discount or coupon applied.","expected_behavior":"The refund amount equals what the customer actually paid (after discount). The coupon/promo code should be re-issued or restored to the customer's account if it was single-use, per business policy."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels during a high-traffic period (e.g., flash sale) and inventory is immediately claimed by another order.","expected_behavior":"Restocking and re-availability are atomic with the cancellation. If a race condition causes overselling, standard oversell handling applies — the cancellation itself must still succeed."},{"scenario":"Order contains items that are no longer in the product catalog (discontinued).","expected_behavior":"Cancellation and refund proceed normally. Inventory for discontinued items may not be restocked to the 'available' pool — it goes to a quarantine or dead-stock bucket depending on business rules."},{"scenario":"Subscription order or recurring order cancellation.","expected_behavior":"Cancelling one instance of a recurring order should not cancel the entire subscription. These are separate bounded contexts and the spec must clarify if subscription management is in scope."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that included a free gift or bundled item.","expected_behavior":"All items in the order — including free/bundled items — are cancelled and restocked. The refund covers only the paid amount."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellations directly reduce revenue but timely refund processing avoids chargebacks (which carry fees and reputation damage). A smooth cancellation flow reduces costly customer service interactions. Incorrect inventory restoration can lead to either lost sales (phantom stock) or overselling (negative customer experience).","operational":"Cancellation must integrate with warehouse management (halt picking/packing), payment processing (initiate refunds), inventory management (restock), and notification systems. Poor integration leads to ghost shipments, double refunds, or inventory drift that compounds over time.","user":"Customers expect self-service cancellation to be instant, frictionless, and clearly communicated. Inability to cancel erodes trust and drives customers to competitors. A clear cancellation UX with honest status communication (e.g., 'too late to cancel, but here's how to return') maintains customer confidence.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) double-refund bugs causing financial loss, (2) race conditions between cancellation and fulfillment causing shipped-but-refunded orders, (3) inventory drift from failed restocking, (4) regulatory/compliance risk if refunds are not processed within legally mandated timeframes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability, reducing frustration and wait times. Clear communication about cancellable windows sets correct expectations."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced cancellation-related ticket volume. Need training on edge cases (post-shipment requests, partial cancellations, failed refunds)."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must handle cancellation signals that halt in-progress picks/packs. Need clear SLAs on when a cancellation can still interrupt fulfillment."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Refund records must reconcile with payment provider statements. Need visibility into cancellation rates and refund totals for reporting."},{"group":"Product/Inventory Management","impact":"Restocked inventory must be accurately reflected. Cancellation rate data informs demand forecasting."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no constraints on cancellation window, partial cancellation policy, refund timing, or state machine. Multiple critical business decisions are left entirely unspecified. The acceptance criteria above represent reasonable industry defaults, but any of them could be wrong for this specific business.","high_confidence_areas":["Orders that have shipped cannot be cancelled (universal logistics constraint)","Cancellation must trigger a refund (legal and financial invariant)","Only the order owner may cancel their order (security invariant)","Cancelled is a terminal state (state machine integrity)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partial cancellation of line items is supported or only full-order cancellation","Exact set of cancellable statuses — is 'Processing' cancellable if picking has started?","Refund timing SLA and whether it varies by payment method","Coupon/promo code restoration policy on cancellation","Whether cancellation reason is required or optional"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle includes states: Pending, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled","Payment capture happens at or before shipment, not at order placement (auth-then-capture model)","Inventory is managed within the same system or has a synchronous integration","This is a standard e-commerce order, not a subscription or marketplace order"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Which order states are cancellable? The spec says 'cancel their order' but does not define when cancellation is permitted. Is an order in 'Processing' (actively being picked in the warehouse) still cancellable? The cutoff point has major operational implications.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Customer Support"],"options":["Allow cancellation only for Pending/Confirmed (before fulfillment starts)","Allow cancellation up to and including Processing (before carrier handoff)","Allow cancellation at any pre-delivery state including Shipped (requiring carrier intercept)"],"recommendation":"Option B — allow cancellation up to carrier handoff. This maximizes customer flexibility while staying within physically achievable bounds. Carrier intercept (Option C) is unreliable and expensive."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Is partial cancellation (cancelling individual line items from a multi-item order) supported, or must the customer cancel the entire order? This significantly affects refund calculation, inventory logic, and UI complexity.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Engineering","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Full-order cancellation only — simpler to implement, customer must cancel entire order and reorder remaining items","Partial cancellation supported — more customer-friendly but requires line-item-level refund calculations and inventory adjustments"],"recommendation":"Start with full-order cancellation only for V1. Add partial cancellation as a fast-follow based on customer feedback and cancellation analytics."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"What is the refund timing SLA? 'Trigger a refund' could mean instant, same-day, or within N business days. Different payment methods have different refund processing times. The spec does not define customer-facing expectations.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Customer Support"],"options":["Refund initiated immediately, customer told to expect 5-10 business days (standard credit card processing)","Refund guaranteed within 24 hours of cancellation","Refund timing varies by payment method with per-method SLA communicated to customer"],"recommendation":"Initiate refund immediately upon cancellation. Communicate payment-method-specific timelines to the customer (e.g., 'Credit card refunds may take 5-10 business days to appear')."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects payment processing, inventory management, fulfillment workflows, and customer communication. The PM's request is a single sentence with no constraints specified, leaving significant room for interpretation.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed

content.thinking:
The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is quite vague. Let me analyze this from a domain perspective and produce comprehensive acceptance criteria.

This is ambiguous - many details are unspecified. Let me classify: this is `ambiguity` because the requirement can be interpreted multiple ways and the spec doesn't clarify which.

Key ambiguities:
- What order states allow cancellation?
- What happens to payment/refunds?
- Is there a time window?
- What about partially fulfilled orders?
- Who is "user" - customer, admin, both?


Now I'm working through the full acceptance criteria to address each of these gaps.

The customer is informed the order has shipped and directed to returns."},{"scenario":"Payment refund fails after order is already marked Cancelled.","expected_behavior":"This must not happen — the order should only transition to Cancelled after refund initiation succeeds (or at minimum, is accepted by the payment provider). If using eventual consistency, a 'CancellationPending' intermediate state should be used, with reconciliation jobs to retry failed refunds."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order paid with multiple payment methods (e.g., gift card + credit card).","expected_behavior":"Refund must be split proportionally across the original payment instruments. Gift card balance is restored, credit card is refunded for the remainder."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that had a promotional discount or coupon applied.","expected_behavior":"The refund amount equals what the customer actually paid (after discount). The coupon/promo code should be re-issued or restored to the customer's account if it was single-use, per business policy."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels during a high-traffic period (e.g., flash sale) and inventory is immediately claimed by another order.","expected_behavior":"Restocking and re-availability are atomic with the cancellation. If a race condition causes overselling, standard oversell handling applies — the cancellation itself must still succeed."},{"scenario":"Order contains items that are no longer in the product catalog (discontinued).","expected_behavior":"Cancellation and refund proceed normally. Inventory for discontinued items may not be restocked to the 'available' pool — it goes to a quarantine or dead-stock bucket depending on business rules."},{"scenario":"Subscription order or recurring order cancellation.","expected_behavior":"Cancelling one instance of a recurring order should not cancel the entire subscription. These are separate bounded contexts and the spec must clarify if subscription management is in scope."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that included a free gift or bundled item.","expected_behavior":"All items in the order — including free/bundled items — are cancelled and restocked. The refund covers only the paid amount."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellations directly reduce revenue but timely refund processing avoids chargebacks (which carry fees and reputation damage).
